title: Enovix Corporation (ENVX) Stock: Q1 Revenue Rises 49% as Investors Watch Cash Burn
text: Enovix reported Q1 2026 revenue of $7.6 million, up 49% year-over-year, driven largely by batteries supplied to Korean military contractors. While revenue growth is strong, investors are monitoring the company's cash burn, which continues at elevated levels. Free cash flow outflow reached $36.3 million in Q1 2026, driven largely by the semiannual interest payment on 2030 convertible notes and higher inventory levels in Korea to support upcoming shipments. Despite cash burn, Enovix maintains a strong cash position with $582.7 million in cash, equivalents, and marketable securities, providing substantial runway for commercialization efforts. Management guided Q2 CapEx to $9-13 million, with higher investments planned for Korea capacity expansion. The company continues to execute on multiple commercialization fronts: smart eyewear battery production ramping, drone/defense applications expanding, and smartphone qualification advancing. While ENVX stock declined after Q1 earnings despite beating expectations, this reflects investor concerns about the extended timeline to smartphone commercialization and continued negative margins ahead of the expected profitability inflection.
